steps.prompt: Which words together form the verb?

hints.est vobis: 'vobis' is a pronoun (for you) — 'Natus est' is the perfect passive of 'nascor'
hints.Natus hodie: 'hodie' is an adverb (today) — the verb is 'Natus est' (was born)
hints.est Salvator: 'Salvator' is a noun (Savior) — the compound verb is 'Natus est'

steps.prompt: The infinitive of 'Natus est' translates to?

hints.to swim: 'to swim' is 'natare/nare' — 'Natus est' comes from 'nasci' (to be born)

hints.Present: Present of 'nasci' would be 'nascitur' — 'Natus est' is the perfect passive
hints.Imperfect: Imperfect would be 'nascebatur' — 'Natus est' (participle + est) is perfect
hints.Future: Future would be 'nascetur' — 'Natus est' is perfect passive (deponent)

hints.nato, natare, natavi, natatum: These are the parts of 'natare' (to swim)
hints.nosco, noscere, novi, notum: These are the parts of 'noscere' (to learn/know)
hints.narro, narrare, narravi, narratum: These are the parts of 'narrare' (to tell/narrate)

hints.Nominative: Nominative of this pronoun is 'vos' — 'vobis' is dative/ablative
hints.Ablative: 'vobis' could be ablative, but here it means 'for you' — dative of advantage
hints.Accusative: Accusative would be 'vos' — 'vobis' is the dative form
Accusative would be 'Salvatorem' — 'Salvator' is nominative, the subject
hints.Genitive: Genitive would be 'Salvatoris' — 'Salvator' is the nominative subject
hints.Vocative: Vocative would be 'Salvator' (same form), but here it is the subject of 'Natus est'

hints.1st declension: 1st declension has genitive in -ae — 'Salvatoris' ends in -is
hints.2nd declension: 2nd declension has genitive in -i — 'Salvatoris' ends in -is
hints.4th declension: 4th declension has genitive in -ūs — 'Salvatoris' ends in -is

translation: A Savior has been born for you today
